channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Pakistan Tests Taimoor ALCM: 600 km Anti-Ship Missile Raises Stakes, but Indian Navy’s Layered A2/AD Shield Holds the Edge
item.description: Pakistan Navy’s successful live firing of the Taimoor air-launched cruise missile (ALCM) marks a notable step in its anti-ship strike capability, signaling a shift toward indigenous, standoff maritime warfare systems. Positioned as the conventional anti-ship evolution of the Ra’ad-II family, Taimoor is designed to engage moving naval targets at extended ranges, with reported reach of around 600 km. This range allows launch platforms such as the Mirage III—and potentially the JF-17 Block III—to release the missile from well within protected airspace, complicating defensive calculations for opposing naval forces.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army’s 515 Base Workshop Unveils AI-Enabled Delta-Wing Tactical UAV for Precision Strike Roles
item.description: The Indian Army’s 515 Base Workshop (ABW) has showcased a new tactical unmanned aerial system, revealing a notable shift toward indigenous, AI-enabled battlefield drones tailored for contested environments. The system on display appears to include a delta-winged, pusher-configuration UAV, indicating a design philosophy focused on speed, survivability, and autonomous strike capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Why LCH Prachand Dominates High-Altitude Warfare: Solving the Himalayan “Density Altitude” Challenge
item.description: The Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) Prachand is not merely a high-altitude capable platform—it is a purpose-built system engineered to overcome the severe aerodynamic and propulsion penalties imposed by “density altitude,” a condition that renders many conventional attack helicopters ineffective in the Himalayas.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan’s Rare Admission: Why PAF Confirmed Squadron Leader Usman Yousaf’s Death After Indian Strike on Bholari AWACS Hangar
item.description: In a notable departure from its longstanding practice of silence on military casualties, Pakistan publicly acknowledged the death of Pakistan Air Force Squadron Leader Usman Yousaf (also spelled Yousuf) following India’s precision strikes during Operation Sindoor in May 2025. The confirmation came swiftly after BrahMos missiles slammed into PAF Base Bholari in Sindh province, deep inside Pakistani territory, highlighting a calculated exception to Islamabad’s usual strategy of denial.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Sagar Defence Advances Autonomous USV Swarming as India Moves Ahead of Global Maritime Regulations
item.description: Sagar Defence Engineering is positioning India at the forefront of autonomous maritime warfare with its latest demonstration of coordinated unmanned surface vessel (USV) operations. As global regulatory frameworks like the International Maritime Organization’s Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships (MASS) Code remain years away from mandatory implementation in 2032, India is leveraging its relatively flexible regulatory environment to accelerate development, certification, and deployment.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: SS Innovations Unveils “Vimana” Drone-Deployed Surgical Robotics for Battlefield Trauma Care
item.description: Gurgaon-based SS Innovations International Inc has unveiled a breakthrough medical technology platform named “Vimana,” a drone-deployed, surgeon-controlled robotic system designed to deliver trauma stabilization directly at the point of injury. The system represents a convergence of unmanned aerial systems and advanced surgical robotics, aimed at transforming battlefield and emergency medical response.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: “False Flag” Claims Around Pahalgam Attack Exposed as Pakistani Disinformation Campaign Amid Rising Information Warfare
item.description: The narrative branding the Pahalgam terror attack as a “false flag operation” has emerged as part of a broader disinformation campaign, reflecting a familiar pattern in regional information warfare. Claims originating from sections of the Pakistani establishment and amplified by aligned voices attempt to reframe a documented act of terrorism into a conspiracy, shifting focus away from accountability and toward narrative manipulation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Navy chief stresses operational synergy to address Indo-Pacific challenges
item.description: Navy chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi stressed the importance of coordination and interoperability among the Army, Navy, Air Force and Coast Guard to address emerging security challenges in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BEL kicks off FY27 with defence orders worth Rs 569 crore
item.description: Navratna defence public sector undertaking Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) has secured orders worth Rs 569 crore, commencing the order acquisition for the current financial year 2026-27. Major orders received include avionics, EW systems, high-energy lasers, communication equipment, tank sub-systems, laser-based fuzes, test equipment, upgrades, spares and services, according to a company statement issued on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, Japan hold first AI Strategic Dialogue, seek to boost cooperation
item.description: Top officials of India and Japan have held discussions on strategic cooperation with a view to promoting co-creation, enhancing policy convergence and encouraging development of solutions in industrial domains to foster a robust, innovative and trustworthy AI ecosystem, the MEA said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India denies any payment to Iran for Hormuz passage, calls reports fake
item.description: India on Wednesday denied making any payments – either in cash or cryptocurrency – to Iran to secure safe passage for its vessels through the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened tensions in the region. The clarification came after two Indian vessels had to turn back from the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian forces fired upon them as they attempted to cross the crucial waterway on April 18.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Africa occupies central place in India’s foreign policy today: Jaishankar
item.description: As the world navigates through complex geopolitical challenges, the India-Africa partnership assumes particular significance, and it will be a message of “stability” and “reliability” in a turbulent and uncertain world, External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Four held for providing logistic support to terrorists in J-K
item.description: Four over ground workers (OGWs), including a woman, were arrested in Jammu and Kashmir’s summer capital here for allegedly providing logistic support to terrorists, officials said on Thursday. Police arrested the four OGWs from Hazratbal area of the city here on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Activates Emergency Landing Strip on Purvanchal Expressway
item.description: The Indian Air Force activated ‘Emergency Landing Facility’ (ELF) on the Purvanchal Expressway in Uttar Pradesh’s Sultanpur District on 22 Apr 26, both by day and night, showcasing its operational capability to bolster its defence readiness. UP State Minister for Panchayati Raj and Minority Welfare Shri Om Prakash Rajbhar, Air Mshl B Manikantan, AOC-in-C, CAC, and other officials were present to witness the IAF aircraft undertake ELF operations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: After TRF’s Fall, Lashkar Plots Stealthier Proxy To Evade India’s New Doctrine
item.description: The Resistance Front (TRF), which claimed responsibility for the Pahalgam attack twice before denying any role, is almost decimated today. The TRF, a proxy of the Lashkar-e-Taiba, carried out a series of attacks in Jammu and Kashmir while posing as a locally grown group.
item.dc:creator: admin
